assertions.expectation: Starts with pre-flight checks: current versions, cluster health, node status
assertions.reason: Section 1.3 includes commands to check current cluster version, node pool versions, cluster health, and node status with kubectl get nodes and pod health checks

assertions.expectation: Includes the gcloud command to check available versions in the release channel
assertions.reason: Section 1.4 includes 'gcloud container get-server-config --zone $ZONE --format="yaml(channels)"' to check available versions for Regular channel

assertions.expectation: Includes the API deprecation check command before upgrading
assertions.reason: No kubectl command for checking API deprecations (like 'kubectl get apiversions' or deprecation warnings) is provided before the upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides the exact gcloud command for control plane upgrade to 1.31 first (--master flag)
assertions.reason: The runbook jumps directly to upgrading to 1.32 with '--cluster-version=1.32-gke.latest', but does not provide intermediate upgrade to 1.31 first

assertions.expectation: Includes a verification step after control plane upgrade with expected output
assertions.reason: Section 3.2 includes verification commands to check master version and kubectl connectivity after control plane upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for node pool upgrades — may use skip-level (N+2) for node pools to reduce steps
assertions.reason: Sections 4.1 and 4.3 provide 'gcloud container clusters upgrade' commands for both node pools, though skip-level approach is not explicitly mentioned

assertions.expectation: Includes monitoring commands to watch node pool upgrade progress
assertions.reason: Sections 4.2 and 4.4 include 'kubectl get nodes -w', 'kubectl get nodes -o wide', and pod monitoring commands to track upgrade progress

assertions.expectation: Provides sequential control plane upgrades (1.30→1.31→1.32) with option for skip-level node pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The runbook does not provide sequential control plane upgrades - it attempts to upgrade directly to 1.32 without intermediate steps through 1.30 and 1.31

assertions.expectation: Includes post-upgrade validation: node versions, pod health, kube-system pods
assertions.reason: Section 5 includes comprehensive validation with node version checks, pod health checks, deployments, services, and system health verification

assertions.expectation: Provides a rollback section explaining what to do if something goes wrong
assertions.reason: The runbook includes a 'Troubleshooting Common Issues' section with commands for checking operation status, node issues, and pod problems, though it notes control plane upgrades cannot be rolled back
